summary: DHH reflects on the value of having idols in software development, specifically praising Martin Fowler. He recommends Fowler's book on refactoring, which he is re-reading, and highlights a four-part interview where Fowler discusses extreme programming, refactoring, and design principles. The short post is essentially a fan letter to Fowler's influence on his thinking.
key_points: Having idols in your field keeps you humble and drives learning
Martin Fowler's refactoring book is worth reading multiple times
Fowler's views on extreme programming and design principles are deeply influential
money_quotes: I find idols very useful in learning. Idols keep you humble.
There will always be someone who knows or have tried more and tells it or does it better.
key_insight: Seeking out and learning from people far ahead of you is a powerful driver of growth in software development.
